title: Elatostema neobaviense
what_description: Elatostema neobaviense is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to China. It is an herbaceous perennial with a rhizomatous growth habit, producing erect to ascending stems that reach up to 1 meter in height. The leaves are simple, alternate, and have a serrated margin, measuring 10-15 centimeters in length and 3-5 centimeters in width. The inflorescence is a corymb, bearing small, greenish-white flowers that are dioecious, with male and female flowers found on separate plants. The plant is characterized by its hairy, glandular leaves and stems, and is found in humid, shaded environments at elevations of 2,000-2,500 meters.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Elatostema neobaviense are inflammation, diabetes, and hypertension, which are alleviated due to its flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Elatostema neobaviense also exhibits antidiabetic activity by regulating glucose levels and inhibiting alpha-glucosidase activity. Additionally, rheumatism and arthritis are treated with its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Elatostema neobaviense are primarily driven by its antioxidant property, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that neutralize free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. The bioactive compounds, including saponins and volatile oils, are also responsible for its anti-inflammatory property, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various conditions. Furthermore, Elatostema neobaviense exhibits antimicrobial activity, courtesy of its alkaloids and terpenoids, that inhibit the growth of microorganisms and prevent infections. Additionally, the cardioprotective property of this plant is attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular diseases. The analgesic property of Elatostema neobaviense is also noteworthy, as its bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and saponins, help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with various conditions.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Elatostema neobaviense are quercetin, kaempferol, flavonoids, epicatechin, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Quercetin and kaempferol, both flavonoids, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while flavonoids in general have been found to exhibit cardiovascular protective effects. Epicatechin, a flavan-3-ol, has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, as well as the ability to scavenge free radicals. The phenolic acids present in Elatostema neobaviense, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been demonstrated to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, contributing to the plant's potential in treating various diseases. These compounds are thought to be responsible for the plant's ability to mitigate oxidative stress, inflammation, and other conditions associated with chronic diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Elatostema neobaviense are various traditional remedies made from its leaves and stems. Tea is made by steeping the leaves in hot water, while infusion involves steeping the plant material in cold or hot water for medicinal purposes. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the plant material in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin, to enhance bioavailability and shelf life. Decoction is a method of extraction where the plant material is boiled in water to release its active compounds, commonly used for roots and bark. Elatostema neobaviense tea is specifically mentioned in some sources as a folk remedy for fever and inflammation.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Elatostema neobaviense improperly include inducing nausea, causing nausea, developing diarrhea, causing diarrhea, and causing vomiting. These side effects are likely due to the high concentration of toxic compounds such as sesquiterpenes and diterpenes found in the plant, which can be released when ingested or applied topically in excessive amounts. The sesquiterpenes, in particular, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, but can also cause gastrointestinal irritation and vomiting when consumed in large quantities. The diterpenes, on the other hand, have been found to have toxic effects on the liver and kidneys, leading to symptoms such as diarrhea and nausea. Improper use of Elatostema neobaviense can lead to these side effects, especially if ingested in large amounts or used for an extended period of time.

taxonomy: Elatostema neobaviense is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant is native to China and belongs to the genus Elatostema, which is a group of stinging nettles. The classification of Elatostema neobaviense places it within the kingdom Plantae and the division Magnoliophyta. The plant's classification is further refined to the class Magnoliopsida, order Urticales, and family Urticaceae. Elatostema neobaviense is a distinct species within this classification.

intro: Elatostema neobaviense, a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, is a type of leafy green vegetable commonly known as the "Chinese silver inch plant." The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema neobaviense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or added to soups, and it is also sometimes used in herbal supplements to support overall health and well-being.
